opp.name: Cascades_CLM_May'26
opp.next_step: 5/19MF - call with Devika, Emilie and Le Hien. Expanded on CLM requirements including ability to manage/redline contracts, MS shop incl Word for contract authoring and MS Teams. They need a better way to manage risk/value leakage on contracts, get visibility into contract utilization, missed renewals and know how much was spent against each contract. They also expressed interest in being able to enforce services rates (maintenance for example) at the contract level. We explained that piece is handled from the P2P downstream component, they're interested in seeing it to understand how to set themselves up for success as Phase 2. CLM + Merlin + P2P overview scheduled for 5/26. 5/11MF - currently have a best of breed patchwork of solutions, different point solutions in place for e-proc, spend analytics, PO confirmation, invoicing, RFx events mgmt and a planned CRM. What he was interested in hearing more about was our CLM capabilities...described as the missing piece. Currently all contracts are stored in Sharepoint. Le Hien (Director, Procurement Excellence) is looking for a CLM solution that will help author contracts, determine whether existing contracts are still acceptable upon renewal and surface / highlight anything that needs to be looked at more closely. Next step set with his extended team (Emilie Noreau and Philippe Granger) and our SC group is scheduled for 5/19. He needs help creating a business case for making a change for budget allocation in FY27 which starts Jan 1.
opp.ais_why:
* Customer-initiated AI demand on the 19-May call: they want to "Layer AI over existing SharePoint library to elevate contract data and enable users to query details and run reports via an AI agent," and called "Automation of contract lifecycle management with AI integration" a priority.
* The AI ask is anchored to concrete CLM pain - no central repository or dashboards, missed/auto renewals, value leakage - so the buyer frames AI/automation as the fix for retrieval, obligation tracking and clause playbooks.
* Still early and single-threaded on CLM: Qualified at 10%, next step is a one-hour demo (May 26); no POC, budget, or executive sponsor yet, so interest has not converted - Curious, not Hungry.

metrics.narrative: The buyer has articulated qualitative pain (risk and value leakage on contracts, missed renewals, lack of visibility into contract utilization and spend, inability to enforce services rates at contract level) across the 11 May and 19 May calls, captured in Next_Step__c. However, no quantified business case exists: no baseline cost of manual contract management, no error rate or leakage estimate, no renewal-miss frequency or financial impact, no FTE burden, no savings target or ROI projection. The Director (LE HIEN DO) explicitly stated he 'needs help creating a business case for making a change for budget allocation in FY27' (Next_Step__c, recorded 19 May), which confirms the gap is recognized but unaddressed 45 days later. Without a defendable number, the deal cannot compete for budget prioritization in a fiscal allocation cycle starting January 2027. The single highest-leverage move is to co-build a quantified ROI model with the Director and present it to the Economic Buyer by mid-August.

He initiated the conversation 11 May, brought his extended team (Emilie Noreau, Philippe Granger) to the 19 May discovery call, attended the 26 May demo, and explicitly stated he needs help building a business case for FY27 budget allocation (Next_Step__c). This demonstrates intent and internal advocacy. However, his champion strength is developing, not strong, because: (1) he has not yet secured CPO (Fabrice Blanc) engagement or sponsorship 53 days into the cycle, (2) no evidence exists that he has positioned Zycus internally against alternatives or sold the vision beyond his immediate team, (3) he has not driven urgency or a formal decision timeline, and (4) his ability to influence budget allocation at the executive level (CPO, CFO) is unproven. His engagement is active (last Salesforce activity 25 June, 9 days ago) but he lacks the access and authority to close this deal alone. The next step is to coach him to secure a CPO briefing, co-develop the quantified ROI case, and position him to advocate for Zycus at the executive level. If he cannot secure that access within 30 days, we must escalate around him through an executive connect (deal owner's manager to CPO).

competition.narrative: The primary competitor is Status Quo / Do Nothing, confirmed across multiple sources. The buyer currently stores all contracts in SharePoint with no dedicated CLM tooling, relying on a 'patchwork of best-of-breed point solutions' for e-proc, spend analytics, PO confirmation, invoicing, and RFx (Next_Step__c, 11 May). The Director described CLM as 'the missing piece,' which implies recognition of the gap but also reveals that the organization has functioned without it and may continue to do so if the business case fails to secure budget approval. Inertia is the wedge: the buyer has no burning platform, no contract expiration forcing function, and no stated compelling event beyond 'FY27 budget allocation.' No external vendor competitor (Icertis, Agiloft, Conga, DocuSign CLM, etc.) has been mentioned in any call, task, or Salesforce field. The Salesforce Competitors__c field is unknown (not populated). The risk is that the deal defaults to status quo if the Director cannot build a defendable ROI case and secure executive sponsorship. The wedge is to quantify the cost of inaction (missed renewals, value leakage, manual FTE burden, risk exposure) and position Zycus iContract + Merlin as the only integrated solution that combines CLM with AI-powered contract intelligence and downstream P2P enforcement, which the buyer expressed interest in (Next_Step__c, 19 May). The activation is to make the ROI case so compelling that doing nothing is indefensible, then lock in CPO sponsorship before any competitor enters the process.

identify_pain.narrative: The buyer has clearly articulated operational pain, driven by the inadequacy of their current state (all contracts stored in SharePoint with no CLM tooling). The pain is multi-dimensional: (1) risk and value leakage on contracts due to lack of visibility and control, (2) missed renewals because no automated tracking or alerting exists, (3) inability to understand contract utilization or spend against each contract, (4) no enforcement mechanism for services rates (e.g. maintenance pricing) at the contract level, and (5) inefficient manual redlining and approval workflows in a Microsoft Word/Teams environment. The Director (LE HIEN DO) described CLM as 'the missing piece' in their procurement stack (Next_Step__c, 11 May) and stated the need to 'determine whether existing contracts are still acceptable upon renewal and surface/highlight anything that needs to be looked at more closely' (Next_Step__c, 11 May). The pain is acute and recognized at the working level. However, the pain owner at the executive level (CPO, CFO, or operational VP) has not been engaged to validate prioritization or quantify the financial impact. The confirmed pain is operational and process-driven; the gap is that it has not been translated into a quantified business case or executive-level urgency.

paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics, legal review, procurement channel, InfoSec requirements, and partner involvement are entirely unknown 53 days into the cycle. No MSA, SOW, or legal stakeholder has been mentioned. No InfoSec or IT approval requirement has surfaced despite the buyer being a Microsoft-centric enterprise (Word, Teams, SharePoint) where integration and data security are typically scrutinized. No SI, partner, or implementation resource has been identified. The Director's focus has been on functional fit and business case development, with no discussion of how contracts are approved, redlined, or signed at Cascades. The only paper-process clue is that the Director stated he needs help building a business case for FY27 budget allocation (Next_Step__c), which implies an internal capital approval process exists but its mechanics are undefined. The next step is to ask the Director to walk through the typical procurement and contracting process for a software purchase of this scope (approval chain, legal review, InfoSec sign-off, MSA vs SOW, signature authority) and identify those stakeholders by name for early engagement.

economic_buyer.narrative: The Economic Buyer is unidentified and unengaged. Fabrice Blanc holds the title Chief Procurement Officer and is listed as a Decision Maker contact role, but has not attended any recorded call (19 May, 26 May) and is not mentioned as a participant or sponsor in Next_Step__c or any task. The Director (LE HIEN DO, Procurement Excellence) is driving discovery and stated he needs help building a business case for FY27 budget allocation, but a Director typically does not control a multi-hundred-thousand-dollar procurement budget approval without CFO or C-level sign-off. No CFO, CEO, or other executive sponsor has been identified. The evidence suggests the CPO (Blanc) may be the Economic Buyer by title, but his lack of engagement 53 days into the cycle is a red flag: either he has delegated full authority to the Director (unlikely for a capital investment), or the deal has not yet reached him (more probable). The path forward is to qualify the approval chain with the Director, then secure a CPO or CFO meeting to validate budget authority, sponsorship, and FY27 prioritization by mid-August.

decision_process.narrative: The decision process is partially visible but not formalized. The Director (LE HIEN DO, Procurement Excellence) initiated the conversation 11 May, brought his extended team (Emilie Noreau, Philippe Granger) to the 19 May discovery call, and attended the 26 May CLM + Merlin + P2P overview demo. This is a working-level evaluation so far, with no executive escalation. The stated timeline is 'FY27 budget allocation' (fiscal year starts 1 Jan 2027), which implies a Q4 2026 budget approval cycle (Nov-Dec). Next_Step__c notes the Director 'needs help creating a business case for making a change for budget allocation in FY27,' confirming the process is: (1) build business case, (2) secure budget approval (likely CPO or CFO), (3) formal vendor selection, (4) contracting. However, no explicit decision deadline, approval authority (single vs committee), or gate criteria has been stated. The CPO (Fabrice Blanc, Decision Maker contact role) has not engaged, and no IT, Legal, InfoSec, or Finance stakeholder has appeared. The risk is that the process is informal and the Director may not have the political capital to drive budget approval without executive sponsorship. The next step is to map the full approval chain (Director → CPO → CFO?) and secure an executive briefing by mid-August to validate the process and timeline.

decision_criteria.narrative: The buyer has articulated functional requirements across two discovery calls (11 May, 19 May) and the 26 May demo, captured in Next_Step__c: CLM with contract authoring, redline management in Microsoft Word, approval workflows via the relevant stakeholder, renewal tracking and automated alerts, visibility into contract utilization and spend against each contract, risk and value-leakage reporting, and interest in downstream P2P enforcement of services rates at contract level. These are use-case-driven and specific. However, no formal evaluation scorecard, RFP criteria, or weighted decision framework has been shared. The buyer is a Microsoft shop (Word, Teams, SharePoint) so native integration is implicitly a must-have. The criteria are confirmed at the working level but not validated by the Economic Buyer or executive sponsor, and no relative prioritization (must-have vs nice-to-have) or vendor comparison rubric exists. The next step is to co-develop a formal evaluation framework with the Director, weight the criteria, and validate it with the CPO before any competitor enters the process.

north_star_verdict.math: Created 12 May 2026, qualified same day. 270 days to close (31 Mar 2027). Sitting in Qualified for 53 days with discovery complete and demo delivered 26 May (39 days ago). Buyer fiscal year starts 1 Jan 2027, 181 days out. To win FY27 budget allocation the business case must be approved by late Q4 2026 (Nov-Dec). Required pace: move to Shortlisted within 30 days, Vendor Selected by mid-September to allow 90+ days for ROI finalization, EB buy-in, and budget approval. Currently behind pace with no EB engagement, no quantified value case, and no visible path to CPO sponsorship. No forward slip recorded yet (first qualified in May, close date aligned to fiscal cycle). Time-in-stage is appropriate for early discovery but the clock on FY27 budget approval is ticking.
evidence: Discovery engagement is active and multi-threaded at working level (Director + procurement team attended 19 May and 26 May calls, last Salesforce activity 25 June, 9 days ago)
Requirements are well-defined (contract authoring, redline management, renewal tracking, risk/value leakage visibility, MS Word/Teams integration, downstream P2P enforcement interest)
Decision Maker contact role (CPO Fabrice Blanc) exists but has never attended a call or engaged directly
No Economic Buyer identified or engaged; no CFO, no executive sponsor beyond the CPO title in Salesforce
No quantified ROI, savings target, or financial impact developed despite Director stating 'needs help creating a business case for FY27 budget allocation' (Next_Step__c)
Competing against status quo (SharePoint + patchwork of point solutions); inertia is the primary risk
Amount ($50k) appears significantly under-scoped for an enterprise CLM + Merlin deployment at a company of Cascades' size, suggesting early placeholder or Phase 1 only
north_star_verdict.headline: Discovery momentum is strong, but the deal lacks the economic foundation and executive sponsorship required to secure FY27 budget approval by January 2027. Without a quantified ROI case and CPO engagement in the next 60 days, this defaults to status quo.

(Source: OpportunityContactRole, Avoma attendee lists, Next_Step__c.)

The Director is building a business case for FY27 budget but has no dollar savings target, headcount reduction, or cycle-time improvement to anchor it. In a competitive budget cycle, an unquantified case loses to initiatives with hard numbers. High-leverage move: co-author the business case with the Director by identifying one or two specific contract misses (an auto-renewal that should have been renegotiated, an under-utilized agreement, a compliance gap) in their existing data and quantifying the cost. This converts the pain from qualitative to financial and gives the Director a defendable number to take to the CPO/CFO. (Source: Avoma 19 May call, no quantified metrics stated.)

No executive sponsor: CPO uninvolved, no C-level champion. Director may lack political capital to secure budget approval without executive backing.

Functional discovery is strong; economic and executive foundation is absent.

competitive_position.summary: No external vendor competition identified. The primary competitor is Status Quo (SharePoint + manual processes + patchwork of point solutions). Buyer has functioned without CLM tooling and may default to inaction if the business case fails to secure FY27 budget approval. Zycus is sole-sourced at the working level but has not yet engaged the Economic Buyer or built the quantified ROI case required to win budget prioritization.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: AI positioning is moderate. Merlin is in scope (Products: iContract;Merlin) and was included in the 26 May demo (CLM + Merlin + P2P overview per Next_Step__c). However, no evidence exists that the buyer has articulated AI appetite, AI use cases, or AI-driven ROI. The Director's stated pain (risk/value leakage, missed renewals, contract utilization visibility) can be addressed by Merlin's contract intelligence and analytics capabilities, but the AI value proposition has not been explicitly positioned or landed. No AIS score, status, or why field is populated. The opportunity is to reframe the business case around AI-powered contract insights (e.g. Merlin surfacing high-risk clauses, auto-flagging renewal deadlines, analyzing spend leakage patterns) and tie it to quantified savings.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Strong functional alignment on CLM core capabilities (contract authoring, redline management, renewal tracking, MS Word/Teams integration, visibility into utilization and spend, risk/value leakage reporting). Buyer is a Microsoft shop and Zycus supports native Word/Teams integration. Buyer expressed interest in downstream P2P enforcement of services rates, which Zycus can deliver via integrated P2P module, creating a Phase 2 expansion path. Gap is on the economic dimension: no quantified ROI case exists to justify the investment, and the $50k opportunity amount appears under-scoped for the described enterprise CLM + Merlin deployment.

hard.ais_why:
* Customer-initiated AI demand on the 19-May call: they want to "Layer AI over existing SharePoint library to elevate contract data and enable users to query details and run reports via an AI agent," and called "Automation of contract lifecycle management with AI integration" a priority.
* The AI ask is anchored to concrete CLM pain - no central repository or dashboards, missed/auto renewals, value leakage - so the buyer frames AI/automation as the fix for retrieval, obligation tracking and clause playbooks.
* Still early and single-threaded on CLM: Qualified at 10%, next step is a one-hour demo (May 26); no POC, budget, or executive sponsor yet, so interest has not converted - Curious, not Hungry.

value.how_we_win: Quantify the cost of inaction (missed renewals, value leakage, manual FTE burden, risk exposure from lack of contract visibility) and position Zycus iContract + Merlin as the only integrated AI-powered CLM that connects contract intelligence to downstream P2P enforcement (which the buyer expressed interest in). Make doing nothing indefensible, then lock in CPO sponsorship before any competitor enters.

value.flag: No quantified ROI or value case: The buyer has articulated clear operational pain (missed renewals, no contract utilization visibility, value leakage, manual processes) but has not quantified the financial cost of the problem or the expected ROI from a CLM solution. The Director is building a business case for FY27 budget but has no dollar savings target, headcount reduction, or cycle-time improvement to anchor it. In a competitive budget cycle, an unquantified case loses to initiatives with hard numbers. High-leverage move: co-author the business case with the Director by identifying one or two specific contract misses (an auto-renewal that should have been renegotiated, an under-utilized agreement, a compliance gap) in their existing data and quantifying the cost. This converts the pain from qualitative to financial and gives the Director a defendable number to take to the CPO/CFO. (Source: Avoma 19 May call, no quantified metrics stated.)

value.flag: Single-threaded at working level with no executive sponsorship: The deal is multi-threaded horizontally (Director + Emilie + Philippe + Veronique) but has zero executive engagement. The CPO has not joined a call, no CFO or SVP has been introduced, and the Director is driving the evaluation alone. In enterprise deals, working-level consensus without executive sponsorship is a leading indicator of no-decision or budget de-prioritization. The buyer is in early Qualified stage (45 days old) and may not see urgency to escalate yet, but without an executive sponsor the deal will stall when budget allocation decisions are made in Q3/Q4 2026. Move: position an executive connect (the deal owner's manager) to engage the CPO or CFO, frame it as a strategic discussion (procurement modernization, AI roadmap, FY27 priorities) rather than a sales call, and use it to validate that CLM is a top-3 priority for FY27. (Source: Avoma attendee lists, OpportunityContactRole, Next_Step__c.)
